items.title: U.S. Identifies Soldier Killed in Incident at Erbil Air Base
items.desc: According to a department statement, Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of Fayetteville, North Carolina, was killed in action on Sunday, during a controlled detonation of a downed one-way unmanned aerial system (UAS) at the base. The incident remains under investigation.

items.title: Syrian Rights Monitor Raises Alarm Over Hidden Camera Campaigns Targeting Syrian Transport Workers
items.desc: SOHR says covert recordings and edited social media videos are being used to shame and incite against transport workers, urging authorities to protect privacy

items.title: Iraqi Security Forces Foil Drone Manufacturing Plot in Baghdad, Arrest Three
items.desc: In a statement, the INSS said the operation was carried out following precise intelligence gathering and under the direct supervision of the investigative judge of the Third Investigative Court, which handles National Security Service cases.

items.title: Prime Minister Barzani, Chinese Consul General Stress Need to Reactivate Kurdistan Parliament
items.desc: The Kurdistan Region's prime minister and China's consul general discussed regional developments, emphasizing stability, stronger bilateral ties, and the need to reactivate Parliament and form a new Kurdistan Regional Government.

items.title: Gulf Football Federation Approves Duhok to Host Gulf Super Cup
items.desc: Following an official inspection, the Gulf Football Federation endorsed Duhok Stadium's readiness to host the Gulf Super Cup, highlighting the Kurdistan Region's growing role in regional football.

items.title: Iraq audit finds delays in corruption cases, financial violations
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s Federal Board of Supreme Audit identified major delays in resolving corruption cases, failures to exercise key legal powers, and widespread financial violations across several government sectors in its 2025 annual report presented to Parliament on Tuesday. items.title: Iraq wheat marketing reaches 4.8 million tons in 2026
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Some 4.8 million tons of locally grown wheat reached Iraq's Trade Ministry storehouses this season, Trade Minister Mustafa Nizar al-Ani said on Tuesday, announcing the close of the 2026 wheat marketing season. items.title: Lebanese army: Israeli forces fired near its units
items.desc: Shafaq News- Beirut The Lebanese army said on Tuesday that Israeli forces opened fire near its units as they carried out a deployment in the town of Zawtar al-Gharbiyah, in the Nabatiyeh district of southern Lebanon. The army reported no casualties or losses from the incident. “The offensive could obstruct deployment steps in the pilot zones being carried out in coordination with the Militar

items.title: Kuwait shuts down power units after Iranian attacks
items.desc: Shafaq News- Kuwait City Kuwait's Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy stated on Tuesday that it had taken several electricity generation units out of service after Iranian attacks targeted power generation and water desalination facilities. The ministry said several electricity generation and water desalination plants came under attack onfor the fourth consecutive day, causing fire

items.title: Iraq urges women to report online extortion
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Communications and Media Commission (CMC), the state body that regulates media and telecommunications, called on citizens on Tuesday to report digital violence against women and any abusive online content targeting them. items.title: Trump slaps 50% tariffs on Canada and Carney vows to 'intensify' trade talks
items.desc: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.title: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
items.desc: Bodycam and dashcam footage shows a police officer rescue a man seconds before his car was engulfed in flames in Texas.

items.title: 'Anti-ICE' army veteran started fire outside Manhattan federal building, say officials
items.desc: Andrew Arrabaca, 43, was armed with two axes, a machete, three knives and fireworks, says the FBI.

items.title: Tropical Storm Bertha threatens US Gulf coast
items.desc: Storm surges up to 4ft are possible along parts of the Louisiana and Mississippi coasts, officials say.

items.title: Tate brothers appear in US court for extradition proceedings
items.desc: They were arrested on Saturday after the UK announced dozens of new charges, including rape and sex trafficking, which they deny.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.desc: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.desc: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: Amid U.S.-Iran war, Houthi rebels threaten to blockade another key strait
items.desc: The Houthis, a Shia militia in Yemen that's aligned with Iran, have announced a naval blockade of a key waterway, the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.

items.title: Spain's World Cup champions return home for parade in front of almost 2 million
items.desc: The players and their coaching staff met with the country's royals, and Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez. An estimated crowd of almost 2 million was on hand to greet the squad through the streets of Madrid.

items.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
items.desc: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, giving players across the Atlantic a chance at winning the same massive jackpot that U.S. players already play for.

items.title: Israeli air strike in Gaza City kills family of six, medics say
items.desc: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in the attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

items.title: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.desc: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.title: Yemen's Houthis announce 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia
items.desc: The Houthis say the embargo begins immediately and is a response to a Saudi blockade of ports and airports in Yemen.
